title: Buttery Syrian White Flour Pilaf
description: A velvety and cozy pilaf dish from Syria featuring white flour and a buttery twist, perfect for a flexitarian-friendly meal anytime of the day.

ingredients.name: White Syrian Flour
ingredients.notes: all-purpose flour or any similar type will do, but Syrian flour has a slightly better taste and texture

instructions: Heat the butter in a large saucepan over medium heat until melted.
Add the chopped onion and saut1e until translucent.
Add the chicken broth, salt, and 2 cups of the white flour, stirring to combine, then cook until the mixture thickens and forms a crust at the bottom of the pan.
Gradually add the remaining 2 cups of white flour, stirring constantly, until the mixture forms a smooth paste similar to dough.
Remove from heat and cover with a clean kitchen towel, letting it rest for 10 minutes.
Uncover, then add the rice and mix until well combined with the dough-like mixture.
Return the mixture to the heat, cook, covered, over low heat for 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and fluffy like a pilaf.
Serve hot, garnished with toasted almonds if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
